codeReview.summary: The fix closes the public LendingCore quote-helper griefing path and strengthens the BatchLiquidation failure-path assertion, but it does not fully resolve the original deployMarketVersion wiring bug. Governed deployments still skip the LENDING_CORE grant and RiskAdapter.quoteViaTicket role mapping, so newly activated governed versions can remain unable to borrow.

issues.description: deployMarketVersion() now performs the LENDING_CORE wiring only when `deployerIsAdmin` is true (lines 141-148), but governed deployments intentionally transfer AccessManager admin to the timelock and revoke it from the deployer (`dualvm/lib/deployment/deployGovernedSystem.ts:132-165`). The governed upgrade flows still call this helper and then register/activate the new version without any follow-up governance-side grant/mapping (`dualvm/scripts/upgradeOracle.ts:27-50`, `dualvm/scripts/liveLiquidationSmoke.ts:45-83`, and the governed path in `dualvm/scripts/liveRiskAdminSmoke.ts:11-23,52-80`). Those versions can therefore still be activated without giving the new LendingCore permission to call RiskAdapter.quoteViaTicket(), so the original blocker from unified-risk-gateway remains unresolved for governed versions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The solidity-worker skill points deployment-helper work at `deploySystem.ts` but does not warn that `deployGovernedSystem.ts` revokes deployer admin, which makes AccessManager admin operations in helpers behave differently under governed fixtures/scripts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/kpa/polkadot/.factory/skills/solidity-worker/SKILL.md:22-26 only calls out `dualvm/lib/deployment/deploySystem.ts`; /home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 691e7f86-9a8e-41b4-b0eb-ba379fc0bc1c records the GovernorLifecycle failure followed by 'My new wiring code tries to call `labelRole` but fails because the deployer is no longer admin'; the handoff at /home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/handoffs/2026-03-17T21-56-32-350Z__arch-scrutiny-fixes__691e7f86-9a8e-41b4-b0eb-ba379fc0bc1c.json:41-45 suggests adding exactly this note to the skill.

summary: Fail: the fix successfully restricts LendingCore's public quote-ticket helpers and improves the BatchLiquidation regression assertion, but it still leaves governed deploy/upgrade flows able to activate versions without the required LENDING_CORE -> RiskAdapter.quoteViaTicket wiring, so the original deployment-helper blocker is not fully resolved.
